purpose: Canonical folder abstraction for repeatable Knowledge Base source intake. Raw folders may keep source-specific names, but inventories and indexes should normalize them into these categories.

canonicalCategories.label: Earnings and quarterly performance material
canonicalCategories.runtimeUse: Near-term performance context, margin trend, and business momentum claims.

canonicalCategories.label: Annual and business reports
canonicalCategories.runtimeUse: Audited operating facts, segment structure, and official annual baseline claims.

canonicalCategories.label: Quarterly and semiannual reports
canonicalCategories.runtimeUse: In-period official reporting, review evidence, and update checks.

canonicalCategories.label: Audit reports
canonicalCategories.runtimeUse: Audited financial-statement evidence and accounting-basis checks.

canonicalCategories.label: Investor presentations and strategy decks
canonicalCategories.runtimeUse: Management narrative, business pipeline, strategy, and capital-allocation context.

canonicalCategories.label: Value-up and shareholder-return material
canonicalCategories.runtimeUse: Capital allocation, value-up, shareholder return, dividend, and buyback claims.

canonicalCategories.label: Governance material
canonicalCategories.runtimeUse: Governance, ownership, board, and meeting-agenda context.

canonicalCategories.label: Sustainability and ESG material
canonicalCategories.runtimeUse: ESG and sustainability context only when explicitly asked or source-backed.

canonicalCategories.label: Source ledgers and collection metadata
canonicalCategories.runtimeUse: Control metadata only. Do not promote directly as runtime evidence.

canonicalCategories.label: Legacy or date-based source package folders
canonicalCategories.runtimeUse: Preserve provenance; normalize file-level document types through inventory.

canonicalCategories.label: Unclassified source material
canonicalCategories.runtimeUse: Manual review required before extraction or claim promotion.

rules: Do not flatten raw files only for visual neatness.
Prefer company folders under each group source package.
Normalize folder names into canonical categories in manifests and indexes.
Treat README, source provenance ledgers, URL ledgers, and _index files as metadata, not source evidence.
A file can remain in a legacy folder if sourceId, companyId, documentType, sourceUrl, and selectionReason are resolved by the ledger.
